items.title: Initial technical baseline created
items.summary: Initial homepage capture created to start tracking UI, UX, and functional updates. This is the baseline reference for all upcoming comparisons.
technicalNotes: This record defines the zero frame for future comparisons.
Editorial text-only changes are excluded from reporting.
Future captures will be evaluated by structural and visual differences.

items.title: Mega-navigation with deep solution taxonomy
items.summary: The homepage exposes a multi-level navigation that organizes platform capabilities by product, solution, environment, and industry, supporting rapid enterprise discovery.
technicalNotes: Navigation supports broad content depth without forcing early page exits.
Information architecture appears optimized for large account buying journeys.

items.title: Hero designed for campaign switching
items.summary: The hero area combines launch messaging, high-visibility CTAs, and media entry points, enabling quick rotation of strategic campaign themes.
technicalNotes: The hero supports both direct conversion and exploratory learning paths.
CTA structure suggests optimization for mixed-intent visitors.

items.title: Capability sections use modular storytelling blocks
items.summary: Core capabilities are presented as repeated sections with a stable pattern: problem framing, concise value statement, product visual, and dedicated CTA.
technicalNotes: Modular content architecture can simplify experimentation and reorder by priority.
Pattern consistency reduces cognitive load across long-scroll experiences.

items.title: Trust layer integrated into conversion journey
items.summary: Social proof and credibility cues are embedded in the same journey as product education, reducing friction between awareness and evaluation stages.
technicalNotes: Trust modules are not isolated in a single section; they reinforce multiple stages.
Layout placement suggests an intent to improve mid-funnel confidence.

items.title: Interaction model favors guided exploration
items.summary: The homepage includes guided interaction patterns such as grouped action links, expandable navigation contexts, and section-level action hooks to keep users moving through the flow.
technicalNotes: Interaction design emphasizes controlled progression rather than free-form browsing.
Future scans should monitor changes to CTA density and section ordering.
